results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.quote: T3SS-1 secreted proteins (effectors) SopE2 and SopB are required for invasion while the SipA protein accelerates entry into cells.
results.note: Abstract only (PMC/ASM full text not retrievable). SipA named as SPI1 T3SS-1 effector accelerating invasion.

results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.note: Full text not retrievable (PMC returned empty body; ASM 403). SipB absent from the abstract; cannot judge from text I could fully retrieve.

results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.note: Full text not retrievable. SipC absent from the abstract; cannot judge from retrievable text.

results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.quote: T3SS-1 secreted proteins (effectors) SopE2 and SopB are required for invasion while the SipA protein accelerates entry into cells.
results.note: SopB (=SigD) named as SPI1 T3SS-1 secreted effector. Abstract only.

results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.quote: We conclude that SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 contribute to serotype Typhimurium invasion of epithelial cells in vitro.
results.note: SopD listed among the SPI1 T3SS-1 effectors contributing to invasion (title + abstract). Abstract only.

results.resolved_title: SipA, SopA, SopB, SopD, and SopE2 Contribute to Salmonella enterica Serotype Typhimurium Invasion of Epithelial Cells
results.quote: T3SS-1 secreted proteins (effectors) SopE2 and SopB are required for invasion while the SipA protein accelerates entry into cells.
results.note: SopE2 named as SPI1 T3SS-1 secreted effector. Abstract only.

results.resolved_title: A Family of Salmonella Type III Secretion Effector Proteins Selectively Targets the NF-kappaB Signaling Pathway to Preserve Host Homeostasis
results.note: Full text read; paper is solely about the PipA/GtgA/GogA effector protease family. SteE (STM1554) is never mentioned. Cited paper does not support SteE.

results.resolved_title: A Family of Salmonella Type III Secretion Effector Proteins Selectively Targets the NF-kappaB Signaling Pathway to Preserve Host Homeostasis
results.quote: a family of related Typhimurium effector proteins, PipA, GogA and GtgA, which are delivered by its type III secretion systems, specifically and redundantly target components of the NF-kappaB signaling pathway
results.note: GtgA named as a S. Typhimurium T3SS-delivered effector.

results.resolved_title: A Family of Salmonella Type III Secretion Effector Proteins Selectively Targets the NF-kappaB Signaling Pathway to Preserve Host Homeostasis
results.quote: a family of related Typhimurium effector proteins, PipA, GogA and GtgA, which are delivered by its type III secretion systems, specifically and redundantly target components of the NF-kappaB signaling pathway
results.note: GogA named as a S. Typhimurium T3SS-delivered effector (encoded within a lysogenic phage).

results.resolved_title: A Family of Salmonella Type III Secretion Effector Proteins Selectively Targets the NF-kappaB Signaling Pathway to Preserve Host Homeostasis
results.note: Full text read; paper covers only PipA/GtgA/GogA. GogB (STM2584) is never mentioned. Cited paper does not support GogB.

Cited DOI ...01115.x did not resolve in PubMed and Wiley returned 402/paywall; could not retrieve any text. NB: the canonical SiiE/SPI4 T1SS paper (Gerlach 2007) is a different DOI ...00919.x; cited DOI appears mis-numbered. INACCESSIBLE, not refuted.

results.resolved_title: Targeting of an enteropathogenic Escherichia coli (EPEC) effector protein to host mitochondria
results.quote: the gene upstream of tir, orf19, encodes an additional type III secreted effector protein. Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB.
results.note: Orf19 = Map, a T3SS-secreted effector of EPEC. Cited row is E. coli O157:H7 Sakai (EHEC); paper studies EPEC but Map is the conserved homolog. Consistent.

results.resolved_title: Targeting of an enteropathogenic Escherichia coli (EPEC) effector protein to host mitochondria
results.quote: the gene upstream of tir, orf19, encodes an additional type III secreted effector protein. Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB.
results.note: Orf19 = Map, T3SS-secreted effector of EPEC E2348/69 (the exact organism studied).

results.resolved_title: VirB/D4-dependent protein translocation from Agrobacterium into plant cells
results.quote: we used fusions between Cre recombinase and VirE2 or VirF to directly demonstrate protein translocation into plant cells. Transport of the proteins ... depended on the VirB/D4 transport system
results.note: VirE2 translocated into plant cells by Agrobacterium VirB/D4 (T4SS). Abstract.

results.resolved_title: VirB/D4-dependent protein translocation from Agrobacterium into plant cells
results.quote: we used fusions between Cre recombinase and VirE2 or VirF to directly demonstrate protein translocation into plant cells. Transport of the proteins ... depended on the VirB/D4 transport system
results.note: VirF translocated into plant cells by Agrobacterium VirB/D4 (T4SS). Abstract.

results.resolved_title: Structure, Dynamics and Cellular Insight Into Novel Substrates of the Legionella pneumophila Type II Secretion System
results.quote: Previous proteomic analysis of culture supernatants ... identified NttE, a 288-amino acid protein encoded by the lpg0189 ORF ... as a secreted substrate of the Legionella T2SS
results.note: NttE (lpg0189) = secreted T2SS substrate of L. pneumophila; paper demonstrates its role and structure.

results.resolved_title: Structure, Dynamics and Cellular Insight Into Novel Substrates of the Legionella pneumophila Type II Secretion System
results.quote: the largest catalog of intact and subdomain substrate structures from any one T2SS; namely LapA, LapB, Map, NttA, NttB, NttC, NttD, NttE, NttG, and the ChiA C-terminal chitinase/mucinase domain
results.note: NttG listed among the named L. pneumophila T2SS substrates (abstract lists NttA-NttG as T2SS substrates).

results.resolved_title: Characterization of apxIVA, a new RTX determinant of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae
results.quote: The apxIVA gene from the serotype 1 A. pleuropneumoniae type strain Shope 4074T encodes a protein ... which has typical features of RTX proteins including hydrophobic domains in the N-terminal half and 24 glycine-rich nonapeptides in the C-terminal half that bind Ca2+.
results.note: ApxIVA is an RTX-family determinant of A. pleuropneumoniae (named organism). RTX C-terminal glycine-rich nonapeptide signal = T1SS substrate hallmark. Abstract frames as RTX toxin; secretion not directly demonstrated in vitro but family/signal supports T1SS.

results.resolved_title: Characterization of apxIVA, a new RTX determinant of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae
results.quote: The apxIVA gene could be detected in all A. pleuropneumoniae serotypes and seems to be species-specific.
results.note: Row organism is Actinobacillus seminis (NCTC10851_00914). The paper studies A. pleuropneumoniae and states apxIVA is species-specific to A. pleuropneumoniae; it makes no claim about A. seminis. Homolog in a different species.

results.resolved_title: SepZ/EspZ Is Secreted and Translocated into HeLa Cells by the Enteropathogenic Escherichia coli Type III Secretion System
results.quote: A SepZ-CyaA fusion was secreted and translocated into HeLa cells in a TTSS-dependent manner. ... we propose to rename SepZ as EspZ.
results.note: EspZ (=SepZ) shown to be a T3SS-secreted/translocated effector of EPEC. Cited row is O157:H7 Sakai; EspZ is LEE-encoded and conserved across A/E pathogens. Abstract.

results.resolved_title: The stability region of the Streptomyces plasmid pIJ101 encodes a DNA-binding protein recognizing a highly conserved short palindromic sequence motif
results.quote: the conjugative plasmid pIJ101 encodes an overlooked protein, SpdA2. We show that SpdA2 is a DNA binding protein which specifically recognizes a palindromic DNA sequence.
results.note: Cited DOI resolves to a Streptomyces plasmid pIJ101 SpdA2 study (conjugation via TraB). No Ralstonia solanacearum, no RipE1, no T3SS effector anywhere. Mis-cited DOI.

results.resolved_title: Vibrio cholerae Requires the Type VI Secretion System Virulence Factor VasX To Kill Dictyostelium discoideum
results.quote: we present evidence that VasH regulates the production of a newly identified protein, VasX, which in turn requires a functional T6SS for secretion. ... We present VasX as a novel virulence factor of the T6SS
results.note: VasX shown to require a functional T6SS for secretion in V. cholerae. Abstract.

results.resolved_title: Extracellular secretion of pectate lyase by the Erwinia chrysanthemi out pathway is dependent upon Sec-mediated export across the inner membrane
results.quote: Erwinia chrysanthemi EC16 secretes several extracellular, plant cell wall-degrading enzymes, including pectate lyase isozyme PelE. ... one hybrid protein was observed to be associated with the outer membrane of E. chrysanthemi in an out gene-dependent manner.
results.note: PelE secreted by the E. chrysanthemi (=Dickeya dadantii) 'out' (T2SS) pathway. Abstract.

results.resolved_title: Innate sensing of HIV-infected cells
results.quote: We examined here how HIV-infected cells are recognized by plasmacytoid dendritic cells (pDCs) and by other cells.
results.note: Cited DOI resolves (PubMed PMID 21379343) to an HIV innate-immunity paper. No Anaplasma phagocytophilum, no AmpA, no T4SS. Mis-cited DOI.

results.resolved_title: Cleavage of osmosensitive transcriptional factor NFAT5 by Coxsackieviral protease 2A promotes viral replication
results.quote: we aimed to understand the mechanism by which NFAT5 interferes with infection of Coxsackievirus B3 (CVB3), a major cause of viral myocarditis.
results.note: Cited DOI resolves (PubMed PMID 29220410) to a Coxsackievirus/NFAT5 paper. No Legionella, no LegC4, no T4SS. Mis-cited DOI.

results.resolved_title: Coxiella burnetii inhibits host immunity by a protein phosphatase adapted from glycolysis
results.quote: CinF has been shown to be translocated into host cells by the Dot/Icm system ... These results indicate that CinF indeed is a substrate of the Dot/Icm system
results.note: CinF (Cbu0513/CBU0041) translocated by the Coxiella burnetii Dot/Icm T4SS; confirmed by beta-lactamase translocation assay.

results.resolved_title: Type VI Secretion System in Pseudomonas aeruginosa: secretion and multimerization of VgrG proteins
results.quote: we characterized three Pseudomonas aeruginosa VgrGs, namely VgrG1a (PA0091), VgrG1b (PA0095), and VgrG1c (PA2685). ... Here, we clearly establish that all three VgrGs are secreted.
results.note: Locus PA0095 = VgrG1b, established as a secreted P. aeruginosa T6SS component (organism field blank in dataset; PA0095 unambiguously P. aeruginosa PAO1).

Cited biorxiv preprint (2025.02.11.637775) not retrievable (403, no PMC, no abstract via PubMed). External sources (PMID 36675258 Tle3/Tli3 T6SS effector/immunity in AIEC E. coli; cascaleslab 'AIEC T6SS Tle3 phospholipase') support Tle3 as a T6SS phospholipase effector in E. coli, but the cited paper itself could not be read.

results.resolved_title: Pseudomonas aeruginosa uses a cyclic-di-GMP-regulated adhesin to reinforce the biofilm extracellular matrix
results.quote: CdrA shares structural similarities to extracellular adhesins that belong to two-partner secretion systems. The cdrA gene is in a two gene operon that also encodes a putative outer membrane transporter, CdrB. ... processed to 150 kDa before secretion into the extracellular medium.
results.note: CdrA (PA4625) = two-partner secretion (T5SS) adhesin of P. aeruginosa PAO1, processed and secreted via transporter CdrB.
